title: trifolium fabaceum
intro: Trifolium fabaceum, also known as Trifolium microphyllum or Microphyllous Clover, is a species of legume in the genus Trifolium. It is a perennial herb with small leaves and purple flowers, commonly found in dry meadows and open woodlands. Trifolium fabaceum has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture, or combined with other herbs in infusions and decoctions to enhance its medicinal properties.
